behavior_summary: U8P_NSBIGWEB::gotHit is a short process invoked when the object instance receives a hit; it immediately destroys the object instance.
The routine takes two parameters (uword, word) that are present but not inspected; no flags, menus, dialogue, or branching occur.
The only observable external effect is a call to Item::destroy(this), which removes the instance from the world.
notable_control_flow: Entry: process runs on hit (process type 0x020C) and proceeds directly to cleanup.
No conditional branches or flag reads/writes; execution is linear and deterministic: hit -> destroy -> end.
No suspension, UI interaction, or internal calls besides destruction.
open_questions: What do the two parameters (uword, word) represent in-game (likely attacker id and damage/flags) and are they ignored intentionally?
Is the immediate destruction unconditional for all hit types (e.g., environmental vs. player attacks) or are callers expected to filter events before invoking this process?
Are there any engine-level side effects or listeners triggered by Item::destroy that rely on this object being removed on hit?

main_function.name: U8P_NSBIGWEB::gotHit
main_function.purpose: Handle an inbound hit/damage event by removing the object instance from the game world.
entry_conditions: Invoked when the object is struck/receives damage (hit event).
Registered as a process of type 0x020C, so it runs in response to an external hit event.

function_map.name: U8P_NSBIGWEB::gotHit
function_map.role: destroy-on-hit handler for this object instance

function_map.name: Item::destroy
function_map.role: engine/item API used to remove or destroy an item/instance

external_calls.purpose: Remove/destroy the current item instance (this) from the game world as cleanup after being hit.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_NSBIGWEB::gotHit is a short process invoked when the object instance receives a hit; it immediately destroys the object instance.
